title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the two photographs and answer the question. You have about one minute.

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what advantages and disadvantages each learning environment might have and how the students might feel.

images.label: A
images.description: A teacher standing at the front of a traditional school classroom with students sitting in rows.

images.label: B
images.description: University students in a large lecture hall taking notes while a professor presents slides.

followUp.question: Which learning environment do you think is the most effective for students? (Why?)

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say how these learning approaches differ and what skills each one might help to develop.

images.label: D
images.description: A group of students working together around a table on a team project.

images.label: E
images.description: A young apprentice learning a practical trade from an experienced mentor in a workshop.

followUp.question: Which approach to learning do you think best prepares people for the workplace? (Why?)
